scope: Port donor model catalog, provider info, and staged feature registry into AgenC's LLM registry layer

requiredBehaviors: store every bundled donor model row, including hidden review-only models
store authoritative per-model context window metadata
store model input modality and tool/search capability hints
preserve default and supported reasoning levels including xhigh
resolve exact, longest-prefix, and single-namespace model identifiers
feed the existing ModelRegistry and StaticModelsManager surfaces
connect the hidden review-only model to the guardian review selector
feed request-shaping metadata such as parallel tool-call support into ModelInfo
edgeCases: newer bundled OpenAI models not present in the previous ad-hoc table
hidden review-only model row stays registered and selectable by guardian review
gpt-5.4 defaults to xhigh rather than being silently downgraded
namespaced model lookup applies prefix matching to the stripped suffix
unknown models continue to use conservative fallback metadata
commands: npm run test --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime -- src/llm/registry/registry.test.ts src/llm/provider.test.ts src/llm/model-registry.test.ts src/llm/models-manager.test.ts src/llm/capabilities.test.ts src/bin/model-facing-tools.test.ts src/agents/role.test.ts src/session/guardian-approval-review.test.ts src/bin/bootstrap.test.ts

requiredBehaviors: centralize built-in provider default model and base URL data
centralize provider API key env var hints
preserve request retry and stream idle timeout defaults
drive provider factory default models, base URLs, and API key labels from the registry
replace ad-hoc provider model catalog constants in config dependencies
replace live metadata provider base URL and API key env defaults
document source provider rows outside AgenC's current runtime provider scope
edgeCases: xai alias normalizes to grok
openai_compatible/custom aliases normalize to openai-compatible
AgenC hosted provider is marked as managed-auth-backed
OpenAI-compatible live metadata uses registry-owned URL and auth defaults
provider factory construction uses registry defaults when env/model overrides are absent
amazon-bedrock is explicitly recorded as out of scope until an AWS SigV4 runtime provider exists

requiredBehaviors: store every donor feature key with AgenC-owned naming
store feature stage and default-enabled metadata
normalize legacy feature keys onto AgenC-owned canonical keys
accept structured feature config entries for multi_agent_v2 and apps_mcp_path_override
bridge staged feature tables into runtime ManagedFeatures during bootstrap
apply configured feature maps on top of defaults
normalize feature dependencies such as code_mode_only requiring code_mode and fanout requiring multi_agent
edgeCases: unknown feature keys are ignored
legacy apply_patch aliases map to apply_patch_freeform
legacy memory and screen-context aliases map to rebranded AgenC keys
apps_mcp_path_override enables when path is present unless enabled is false
multi_agent_v2 config fields are accepted while enablement still follows enabled
multi_agent_v2 does not imply the older multi_agent flag
bootstrap config tables can disable apps and enable legacy Landlock through ManagedFeatures
removed compatibility flags are known without re-enabling deleted behavior
experimental feature specs are discoverable for menus
